event_text: English colonists establish Jamestown in Virginia, the first permanent English settlement in North America.
The first representative legislative assembly in English North America meets at Jamestown.
The first recorded Africans arrive in English North America at Point Comfort in Virginia.
The Mayflower Compact is signed before the Pilgrims establish Plymouth Colony.
New Amsterdam surrenders to English forces and is later renamed New York.
King Charles II grants William Penn the charter for Pennsylvania.
The Salem witchcraft crisis begins in colonial Massachusetts.
James Oglethorpe and settlers found the colony of Georgia at Savannah.
The French and Indian War begins with fighting near present-day Uniontown, Pennsylvania.
The Treaty of Paris ends the French and Indian War and reshapes Britain’s North American empire.
The British Parliament passes the Stamp Act, increasing colonial opposition to imperial taxation.
British soldiers fire on civilians in Boston in an event remembered as the Boston Massacre.
Colonists protest British tea policy by dumping tea into Boston Harbour.
The First Continental Congress meets in Philadelphia.
Fighting at Lexington and Concord begins the American Revolutionary War.
The Second Continental Congress convenes in Philadelphia.
The Declaration of Independence is adopted.
George Washington crosses the Delaware River before the Battle of Trenton.
The Continental Congress adopts the Articles of Confederation.
The United States and France sign the Treaty of Alliance.
The Articles of Confederation go into effect.
British forces surrender at Yorktown, effectively ending major fighting in the Revolutionary War.
The Treaty of Paris formally ends the American Revolutionary War.
The Constitutional Convention opens in Philadelphia.
Delegates sign the United States Constitution in Philadelphia.
New Hampshire becomes the ninth state to ratify the Constitution, allowing it to take effect.
George Washington is inaugurated as the first President of the United States.
Congress proposes amendments that become the Bill of Rights.
The Bill of Rights is ratified.
President Washington lays the cornerstone of the United States Capitol.
The federal government suppresses the Whiskey Rebellion in western Pennsylvania.
John Adams becomes the second President of the United States in the first peaceful transfer of presidential power.
Congress establishes the Library of Congress.
The District of Columbia comes under the jurisdiction of the United States Congress.
The Louisiana Purchase Treaty is signed, doubling the size of the United States.
The Lewis and Clark Expedition leaves Camp Dubois to explore the Louisiana Purchase.
The United States declares war on Great Britain, beginning the War of 1812.
British troops burn public buildings in Washington, D.C. during the War of 1812.
American forces defeat the British at the Battle of New Orleans.
The Missouri Compromise is enacted, admitting Maine and setting conditions for Missouri’s admission.
President James Monroe announces the Monroe Doctrine.
President Andrew Jackson signs the Indian Removal Act.
Nat Turner leads a rebellion against slavery in Virginia.
The Battle of the Alamo ends during the Texas Revolution.
The Second Seminole War begins in Florida.
Federal removal of the Cherokee people begins when US troops start rounding up Cherokee families for forced relocation westward on the Trail of Tears.
Samuel Morse sends the first long-distance telegraph message from Washington to Baltimore.
Texas is admitted to the United States as the 28th state.
The United States declares war on Mexico.
Gold is discovered at Sutter’s Mill in California.
The Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo ends the Mexican-American War.
The Seneca Falls Convention begins, advancing the women’s rights movement.
California is admitted to the United States as the 31st state.
The Fugitive Slave Act becomes law as part of the Compromise of 1850.
The Compromise of 1850 reshapes the national debate over slavery and expansion.
The Kansas-Nebraska Act becomes law, intensifying conflict over slavery in the territories.
The Supreme Court issues the Dred Scott decision.
John Brown’s raid on Harpers Ferry begins.
Abraham Lincoln is elected President of the United States.
South Carolina secedes from the United States.
Confederate forces fire on Fort Sumter and the American Civil War begins.
President Abraham Lincoln signs the Homestead Act.
President Abraham Lincoln issues the Emancipation Proclamation.
The Battle of Gettysburg is fought in Pennsylvania.
President Abraham Lincoln delivers the Gettysburg Address.
General Robert E. Lee surrenders to General Ulysses S. Grant at Appomattox Court House.
President Abraham Lincoln is shot at Ford’s Theatre.
The 13th Amendment abolishes slavery in the United States.
The 14th Amendment is ratified, defining citizenship and equal protection.
The first transcontinental railroad is completed at Promontory Summit, Utah.
The 15th Amendment is ratified, prohibiting denial of voting rights based on race.
The Battle of the Little Bighorn takes place in Montana Territory.
President James A. Garfield is shot in Washington, D.C.
The Statue of Liberty is dedicated in New York Harbour.
U.S. Army soldiers kill Lakota people at Wounded Knee.
Ellis Island opens as a federal immigration station.
The Supreme Court issues the Plessy v. Ferguson decision.
The United States declares war on Spain.
The Spanish-American War armistice is signed.
Theodore Roosevelt becomes President after the death of William McKinley.
The Wright brothers make the first controlled powered airplane flights at Kitty Hawk.
A major earthquake strikes San Francisco.
The Pure Food and Drug Act is signed into law.
The National Association for the Advancement of Colored People is founded.
The 17th Amendment establishes direct election of United States senators.
The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand helps trigger the First World War.
The United States enters the First World War.
The First World War armistice takes effect.
The 18th Amendment is ratified, leading to national Prohibition.
The 19th Amendment is ratified, recognising women’s right to vote.
KDKA in Pittsburgh makes one of the first commercial radio broadcasts in the United States.
The Chinese Exclusion Act is extended through the Immigration Act of 1921 era of restrictive quotas.
The Wall Street crash begins with Black Thursday.
Black Tuesday marks a deeper collapse of the stock market.
Franklin D. Roosevelt is inaugurated during the Great Depression.
The 21st Amendment is ratified, ending national Prohibition.
President Franklin D. Roosevelt signs the Social Security Act.
Japan attacks Pearl Harbor and the United States enters the Second World War.
Executive Order 9066 authorises the forced removal and incarceration of Japanese Americans.
Allied forces land in Normandy on D-Day.
The Servicemen’s Readjustment Act, known as the GI Bill, is signed into law.
Victory in Europe Day marks the defeat of Nazi Germany.
The United States drops an atomic bomb on Hiroshima.
The United States drops an atomic bomb on Nagasaki.
Japan formally surrenders and the Second World War ends.
The United Nations comes into existence.
President Harry Truman asks Congress to support what becomes known as the Truman Doctrine.
President Harry Truman signs the Economic Cooperation Act, launching the Marshall Plan.
The Korean War armistice is signed.
The Supreme Court decides Brown v. Board of Education.
Rosa Parks is arrested in Montgomery, Alabama, sparking the bus boycott.
President Dwight Eisenhower signs the Federal-Aid Highway Act.
The Soviet launch of Sputnik begins the Space Age and intensifies the U.S. space race.
Explorer 1 becomes the first successful U.S. satellite.
The National Aeronautics and Space Administration is created.
The Greensboro sit-ins begin at a Woolworth’s lunch counter in North Carolina.
Alan Shepard becomes the first American in space.
John Glenn becomes the first American to orbit Earth.
The March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom is held.
President John F. Kennedy is assassinated in Dallas, Texas.
President Lyndon Johnson signs the Civil Rights Act of 1964.
President Lyndon Johnson signs the Voting Rights Act of 1965.
Medicare and Medicaid are signed into law.
Martin Luther King Jr. is assassinated in Memphis, Tennessee.
Apollo 11 astronauts land on the Moon.
The first Earth Day is observed in the United States.
The 26th Amendment lowers the voting age to 18.
The Watergate break-in takes place in Washington, D.C.
President Richard Nixon resigns from office.
Saigon falls and the Vietnam War ends.
The United States marks the bicentennial of the Declaration of Independence.
The Three Mile Island nuclear accident begins in Pennsylvania.
Iranian militants seize the U.S. embassy in Tehran, beginning the Iran hostage crisis.
Space shuttle Columbia launches on STS-1, the first space shuttle mission.
Sally Ride becomes the first American woman in space.
The space shuttle Challenger is destroyed after launch.
The Berlin Wall falls, signalling a major turning point in the Cold War.
President George H. W. Bush signs the Americans with Disabilities Act.
Operation Desert Storm begins during the Gulf War.
The Hubble Space Telescope is launched aboard space shuttle Discovery.
A terrorist bombing damages the World Trade Center in New York.
The Oklahoma City bombing kills 168 people.
Terrorist attacks strike New York City, Washington, D.C. and Pennsylvania.
The USA PATRIOT Act is signed into law.
The Iraq War begins with the U.S.-led invasion of Iraq.
Hurricane Katrina makes landfall on the Gulf Coast.
Barack Obama is elected President of the United States.
The Affordable Care Act is signed into law.
U.S. forces kill Osama bin Laden in Pakistan.
The Supreme Court decides Obergefell v. Hodges, recognising same-sex marriage nationwide.
A mass shooting at Pulse nightclub in Orlando kills 49 people.
Donald Trump is inaugurated as President of the United States.
A national emergency is declared in response to COVID-19.
A mob attacks the United States Capitol during the certification of the 2020 presidential election.
Juneteenth National Independence Day becomes a federal holiday.
The Supreme Court decides Dobbs v. Jackson Women’s Health Organization.
The Inflation Reduction Act is signed into law.
A total solar eclipse crosses the United States.
